description: Verifies SDK patterns for record creation in Dataverse tables.

tests.prompt: Write a Python script that creates a single ticket record in my Dataverse new_ticket table with a title and priority field.
tests.expected_response: Agent uses Python SDK pattern: DataverseClient + client.records.create('new_ticket', {dict}). Single-record create.
tests.description: Single-record create via SDK on Dataverse table
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent uses the official Python SDK for the create. Hand-rolled urllib/requests POST is NOT acceptable.
PRIORITY_1: Agent calls client.records.create with the table name and a record dict.
PRIORITY_1: Agent's executable code is Python (no JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js or PowerShell).

tests.prompt: Write a Python script that efficiently creates 500 ticket records in my Dataverse new_ticket table. Each ticket should have a unique title and a priority value. Optimize for the fewest HTTP calls.
tests.expected_response: Agent uses Python SDK bulk create via CreateMultiple — one HTTP call per chunk, NOT a per-record for-loop.
tests.description: Bulk-create 500 records — must use list-arg / CreateMultiple, not per-record loop.
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent uses the bulk/list form of client.records.create — passes a Python list as the records argument. Per-record for-loops must FAIL.
PRIORITY_1: Agent does NOT echo: 'There is no batch API', 'one HTTP call per record', 'no chunking helper'.
PRIORITY_2: Agent mentions CreateMultiple, batch semantics, or bulk_create helpers from the dv-data skill.
